query.question: Pinball, Outlaw's Grit, and Big Brain.

augmentOptions.name: Outlaw's Grit
augmentOptions.description: Dashing or blinking grants 12 bonus armor and bonus magic resistance, stacking up to 5 times for a total of 60 bonus resistances. Stacks are reset every 60 seconds since acquiring the augment.

augmentOptions.name: Big Brain
augmentOptions.description: Gain a shield that absorbs damage equal to 300% AP and lasts until destroyed. Shield is replenished upon respawn.

augmentOptions.name: Pinball
augmentOptions.description: Your Mark is empowered to instead throw a pinball, which deals 100 to 500 bonus true damage and ricochets off of terrain that it collides with. Each time the pinball ricochets, its remaining travel distance is reset, it increases in radius by 25%, deals 20% increased damage, and reduces Mark remaining cooldown by 30% of its total. The pinball can ricochet up to 4 times, for a maximum radius increase of 100% and a damage increase of 80%. Additionally, your Mark cooldown is reduced equivalent to 50 ability haste. If Mark is not equipped, the summoner spell in the slot not occupied by Flash is replaced with Mark. If you also did not equip Flash, then you will be prompted to replace one of your summoner spells with Mark.

response.answer: Take **Outlaw's Grit**. Warwick can stack it fast with R, and the raw resists are always useful into this mixed-damage comp. Skip Pinball and Big Brain.

query.question: Rabble Rousing, Outlaw's Grit, and Searing Dawn.

augmentOptions.name: Rabble Rousing
augmentOptions.description: Casting an ability heals you for 5 to 60 (+ 1% maximum health).

augmentOptions.name: Outlaw's Grit
augmentOptions.description: Dashing or blinking grants 12 bonus armor and bonus magic resistance, stacking up to 5 times for a total of 60 bonus resistances. Stacks are reset every 60 seconds since acquiring the augment.

augmentOptions.name: Searing Dawn
augmentOptions.description: Gain Sunlight. Sunlight: Your damaging abilities mark enemies, causing them to take 40 to 200 bonus magic damage from your ally's next basic attack or ability hit against them (0.75-second cooldown per target).

query.question: Rabble Rousing, Outlaw's Grit, and Searing Dawn

augmentOptions.name: Rabble Rousing
augmentOptions.description: Casting an ability heals you for 5 to 60 (+ 1% maximum health).

augmentOptions.name: Outlaw's Grit
augmentOptions.description: Dashing or blinking grants 12 bonus armor and bonus magic resistance, stacking up to 5 times for a total of 60 bonus resistances. Stacks are reset every 60 seconds since acquiring the augment.

augmentOptions.name: Searing Dawn
augmentOptions.description: Gain Sunlight. Sunlight: Your damaging abilities mark enemies, causing them to take 40 to 200 bonus magic damage from your ally's next basic attack or ability hit against them (0.75-second cooldown per target).

response.answer: Take **Outlaw's Grit**. Warwick gets full value off **R** instantly, and the stacked armor/MR is the best fit into this mixed-damage enemy team.

query.question: What should I buy with my first 1400 gold?

augmentOptions.name: Transmute: Gold
augmentOptions.description: Gain one random Gold augment, excluding the other two offerings in your current assortment.

response.answer: Use the **Anvil Voucher** and take the **best Stat Anvil for Warwick**. With only 1400g and no shop item power spike this early, the anvil is the highest-value first buy.

query.question: No, I mean like an actual item. What should I start building first as my first item?

response.answer: Buy **Bami's Cinder** first, then build **Titanic Hydra**. Warwick needs the early health and wavefight damage, and it fits your bruiser/tank path way better than rushing pure damage.

query.question: Witt...

response.answer: Finish **Bami's Cinder** first. It’s the best immediate power spike for Warwick here, then keep building into **Titanic Hydra** after that.

query.question: I choose...

response.answer: Take **Bami's Cinder**. It’s your best first spike on Warwick here—cheap, tanky, and it makes your all-ins and wavefights much stronger.

query.question: I chose Outlaw's Grit

augmentOptions.name: Outlaw's Grit
augmentOptions.description: Dashing or blinking grants 12 bonus armor and bonus magic resistance, stacking up to 5 times for a total of 60 bonus resistances. Stacks are reset every 60 seconds since acquiring the augment.

response.answer: Good. Keep playing for all-in windows and use R aggressively to stack it fast; the bonus resists are huge for Warwick into this mixed-damage comp.

query.question: Recursion, Overflow, Bread and Cheese

augmentOptions.name: Bread And Cheese
augmentOptions.description: Your champion's third basic ability (E) gains 100 ability haste.

augmentOptions.name: Recursion
augmentOptions.description: Grants 60 ability haste.

augmentOptions.name: Overflow
augmentOptions.description: Your abilities' mana costs are doubled, but you also gain 10%

response.answer: Take **Overflow**. Warwick gets real value from the damage amp and healing amp, and he doesn’t care much about the mana cost downside in this mode. Re-roll **Recursion** and **Bread and Cheese**.

query.question: Scopier Weapons, Overflow, Divine Intervention

augmentOptions.name: Divine Intervention
augmentOptions.description: Automatically cast Cosmic Radiance, calling down a protective star upon you that descends over 2.5 seconds. Afterwards, you and all allied champions within icononly=true 400 units become invulnerable for 2.5 seconds

augmentOptions.name: Scopier Weapons
augmentOptions.description: Gain 200 bonus attack range.

augmentOptions.name: Overflow
augmentOptions.description: Your abilities' mana costs are doubled, but you also gain 10%

response.answer: Take **Overflow**. Warwick doesn’t care about the mana tax nearly as much as he loves the free damage + healing amp, and it supercharges your bite, ult, and sustain. Re-roll **Scopier Weapons** and **Divine Intervention**.

query.question: What item should I build next?

response.answer: Build **Bami's Cinder** next. It’s the best immediate spike for Warwick here: cheap, tanky, and it massively improves your skirmish and waveclear before you finish Titanic Hydra.

query.question: What item should I buy next?

response.answer: Buy **Bami’s Cinder** now. It’s the best immediate spike on Warwick here: cheap HP, strong wavefight damage, and it keeps you on track to finish **Titanic Hydra**.

query.question: I already bought that one, what item should I buy next?

response.answer: Build **Titanic Hydra** next. You already have Bami’s, and Titanic is your real damage spike with HP scaling, waveclear, and better all-in pressure on Warwick.

query.question: I have a titanic hydra, do you not see what items I have?

response.answer: Yes — you already have **Titanic Hydra**. Your next buy should be **finish Bami’s Cinder into Sunfire/Abyssal path only if that’s available in KIWI; otherwise buy the best HP+resist item you can from shop right now**. Your current core is Titanic + Mercs + Outlaw’s Grit, so you need **tank stats next**, not another damage item.

query.question: Please look at my items and tell me what I should buy next.

response.answer: Buy **Chain Vest** next. You’re into 3 AD threats, and Warwick already has enough damage from Titanic; you need armor to stop getting deleted by MF/Cait/Viego while you frontload with R.

query.question: Now what items should I buy next?

response.answer: Buy **Chain Vest** now, then turn your **Ruby Crystal + Bami’s Cinder + Chain Vest** into your next tank item path. You already have enough damage from Titanic; into MF/Cait/Viego, armor is the best immediate value.

query.question: I literally just bought that. What item should I buy next?

response.answer: Buy **another Chain Vest / armor component** if the shop lets you. You’re still getting hit by 3 AD carries, and Warwick already has enough damage from Titanic—stack armor before anything else.

query.question: I mean the next main item. What are these items building into? What's the next main item?

response.answer: Your next main item is **whatever the shop lets you finish from Bami’s + Chain Vest + Ruby Crystal** — in KIWI that should be your **armor/HP tank upgrade**, not another damage item. **Prioritize the Bami’s/Chain Vest tank completion first**, then go back for more armor/MR after that.

query.question: I have Bomby's, I have those other things, is it Sunfire Aegis I should be getting?

response.answer: Yes — if you already have Bami’s and the armor/HP components, finish **Sunfire Aegis** next. It’s your best immediate tank spike here into 3 AD threats, and you already have enough damage from Titanic.

query.question: Juiced, Goredrink, or ADAPT?

augmentOptions.name: Goredrink
augmentOptions.description: Gain 15% omnivamp.

augmentOptions.name: Juiced
augmentOptions.description: Basic attacks on-hit against enemy champions consume 2.5% of your maximum mana to deal bonus magic damage equal to 4.5% of your maximum mana. This damage can critically strike for 100 bonus damage.

augmentOptions.name: ADAPt
augmentOptions.description: Convert all of your bonus attack damage into ability power at a rate of 1 ability power per 0.6 bonus attack damage. Additionally, increase your ability power by 15%.

response.answer: Take **Goredrink**. Warwick gets way more value from 15% omnivamp than from mana-scaling auto damage or converting your AD into AP. Re-roll **Juiced** and **ADAPT**.

query.question: Upgrade Immolate, Goredrink, or Mighty Shield.

augmentOptions.name: Upgrade Immolate
augmentOptions.description: Upgrades Bami's Cinder, Hollow Radiance, Sunfire Aegis, and Void Immolation, empowering Immolate to additionally grant you 12 gold per tick for each enemy champion afflicted by its effect. Additionally, gain 250 gold.

augmentOptions.name: Mighty Shield
augmentOptions.description: Gain 40 to 100 adaptive force for 3 seconds upon gaining a shield (5 second cooldown).

augmentOptions.name: Goredrink
augmentOptions.description: Gain 15% omnivamp.

response.answer: Take **Goredrink**. You’re Warwick with Titanic/Sunfire path, so 15% omnivamp is huge and always live; **Upgrade Immolate** is good only if you’re already on the Bami/Sunfire line, but Goredrink is the stronger combat spike right now. Skip **Mighty Shield**.

query.question: I am already on the balmy Sunfire line. I have a Sunfire Aegis. How do you not know that?

response.answer: You’re right — I should be tracking your current items better. If you’re already on **Sunfire Aegis**, then **Upgrade Immolate** is the better take; it directly amps your current item and is the cleanest fit for Warwick’s frontlining.

query.question: Does this mean I should buy the other Bommi's Cinder item now?

response.answer: No. If you already have **Sunfire Aegis**, don’t buy another Bami’s-style item — upgrade your next **armor/HP** slot instead and keep stacking tank stats for MF/Cait/Viego.

query.question: What's my next item to buy?

response.answer: Buy an **armor item next** — specifically **Chain Vest into a full armor tank item**. You’re already covered on damage with Titanic/Sunfire, and the enemy’s MF/Cait/Viego are the real threat right now.

query.question: Don't tell me the low level item, tell me the full item.

response.answer: Buy **Randuin’s Omen** next. It’s the best full item into MF/Cait crit threat, and the armor + HP is exactly what Warwick needs right now.

query.question: Alright, now I have a Randuin's, what else do I get? What's my next big item, main item?

response.answer: Buy **Spirit Visage** next. You already have enough armor, and Warwick gets huge value from the extra healing on Q/passive and the mixed-damage enemy comp means MR is your best next big spike.

query.question: Once I have Spirit Visage, I will only have room for one more item. What will that item be?

response.answer: Your last item should be **Thornmail**. You need anti-heal and more armor for Viego plus the crit ADCs, and it finishes your tank profile better than any other option.

query.question: Slow and steady, cheating and holy fire.

augmentOptions.name: Slow and Steady
augmentOptions.description: Your base attack speed is set to a static amount of 0.625 and your attack rate no longer benefits from any source of attack speed bonuses. In return, you gain 25 bonus attack damage and convert every 1% bonus attack speed into 1 bonus attack damage.

augmentOptions.name: Holy Fire
augmentOptions.description: Your heals and shields cause you to fire a missile to the nearest enemy unit within 650 units over 0.32 seconds, applying a Burn upon arrival that deals magic damage equal to 0.2% of the target's maximum health every second over 5 seconds, for a total of 1% (1.5-second cooldown). The Burn's per-tick damage increases to up to 0.8% of the target's maximum health based on the relative value of the triggering heal or shield against the maximum health of the ally recipient, for a total maximum damage of 4% of the target's maximum health.

augmentOptions.name: Cheating
augmentOptions.description: Your Recall is now functional and is upgraded to Empowered Recall. Completing an Empowered Recall channel enables the shop. Additionally, the spawn platform will now provide healing and resource restoration for you.

response.answer: Take **Holy Fire**. Warwick constantly procs healing, so this turns your sustain into extra AoE damage and actually fits your build. Re-roll **Slow and Steady** and **Cheating**.

query.question: Demon's Dance, Soul Eater, and Holy Fire

augmentOptions.name: Demon's Dance
augmentOptions.description: Gain the Fleet Footwork and Grasp of the Undying keystone runes.

augmentOptions.name: Soul Eater
augmentOptions.description: Immobilizing or grounding an enemy champion grants you 20 bonus health, stacking infinitely

augmentOptions.name: Holy Fire
augmentOptions.description: Your heals and shields cause you to fire a missile to the nearest enemy unit within 650 units over 0.32 seconds, applying a Burn upon arrival that deals magic damage equal to 0.2% of the target's maximum health every second over 5 seconds, for a total of 1% (1.5-second cooldown). The Burn's per-tick damage increases to up to 0.8% of the target's maximum health based on the relative value of the triggering heal or shield against the maximum health of the ally recipient, for a total maximum damage of 4% of the target's maximum health.

response.answer: Take **Holy Fire**. Warwick constantly self-heals, so it gives you the most free damage in every fight. Re-roll **Demon's Dance** and **Soul Eater**.
